title: NULL cannot automatically be casted to every other type
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Test Entry');
SELECT id, name FROM test_table UNION SELECT CAST(NULL AS INT), CAST(NULL AS VARCHAR(50));
CREATE TABLE another_table (value FLOAT, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO another_table (value, description) VALUES (CAST(NULL AS FLOAT), 'Null Value Test');
SELECT value, description FROM another_table UNION SELECT CAST(NULL AS FLOAT), CAST(NULL AS VARCHAR(255));
key_bug_features: NULL casting issue in union queries
